channel.title: UMBRASEC Research
channel.description: Independent defensive security research - detection engineering, threat analysis, and open-source tooling.

item.title: Detecting the LiteLLM Command Injection (CVE-2026-42271) in Your AI Gateway
item.description: CVE-2026-42271 turns LiteLLM's MCP preview endpoints into authenticated command execution on the proxy host, and chains with the Starlette "BadHost" bug (CVE-2026-48710) into unauthenticated RCE. In CISA KEV and actively exploited - the mechanism at a defender's depth, what to log, and two runnable Sigma rules with tuning notes.

item.title: Prompt Injection and the OWASP LLM Top 10: A Field Guide for Defenders
item.description: The OWASP LLM Top 10 read as a defender's checklist: why prompt injection has no patch, the "lethal trifecta" behind incidents like EchoLeak (CVE-2025-32711), what to log in LLM-integrated apps, and canary, egress, and behavioral detections you can deploy today.

item.title: Detecting OAuth Consent Phishing in Microsoft 365
item.description: The phishing class that never asks for a password and sails through MFA - illicit OAuth consent grants (MITRE ATT&CK T1528) in Microsoft Entra ID, the audit-log artifacts they leave, and KQL detections with tuning notes.

item.title: Detecting Kerberoasting: A Practical Walkthrough with Sigma
item.description: How Kerberoasting (MITRE ATT&CK T1558.003) works, why RC4 service tickets give it away, and three layered Sigma detections - RC4 downgrade, request fan-out, and a honeypot SPN - with tuning and false-positive notes you can run against your own logs.
